subject: Cloud Computing
title: Azure's Enterprise Strategy
description: A short lesson to help you apply Azure's Enterprise Strategy.

hook.text: Microsoft was late to cloud. But it had something AWS didn't: relationships with every enterprise IT department.

buildup.text: Azure launched in 2010. Microsoft bundled it with existing Office and Windows enterprise contracts.

discovery.text: Enterprises already paying for Microsoft products got Azure discounts. Switching was the easy choice.

twist.text: Azure's strategy wasn't better tech — it was better sales. Enterprise trust took decades to build.

climax.text: Azure now holds about 24% of the cloud market and is the fastest-growing major cloud platform.

punchline.text: Azure won enterprises not with tech but with trust.

quiz.question: How did Microsoft Azure gain enterprise market share despite launching late?
options: By leveraging existing enterprise relationships and bundling with Microsoft products
By offering the lowest prices in the cloud market
By acquiring AWS and merging the two platforms
